title: Iliad, Book 8: The Trojans at the Wall
description: Book 8 of Homer's Iliad: The Trojans at the Wall.
introduction: Book 8 of the Iliad: The Trojans at the Wall. The Greek text is from the Venetus A manuscript (10th century), the oldest complete manuscript of the Iliad.

translation.original_text: Ἠὼς μὲν κροκόπεπλος ἐκίδνατο πᾶσαν ἐπ᾿ αἶαν.
translation.text: Dawn in her saffron robe spread over the earth. Zeus forbade the gods to intervene, and the Trojans pushed the Greeks back to their ships. Hector drove them to the very wall of their camp, and the Trojans camped on the plain that night with a thousand fires burning.
